channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: COAS passes out 3,000 Army recruits in Ebonyi
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Chief of Army Staff passed out over 3,000 new Army recruits in Ebonyi, boosting national defence. Learn about Nigeria’s efforts to strengthen its milit
Read More: https://punchng.com/coas-passes-out-3000-army-recruits-in-ebonyi/

item.title: Reps reject call to summon Tinubu over constituency projects funding
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The House of Representatives rejected a proposal to summon President Tinubu to explain the alleged suspension of funding for constituency projects. Read mo
Read More: https://punchng.com/reps-reject-call-to-summon-tinubu-over-constituency-projects-funding/

item.title: PFIPC scandal: ‘Fake’ DG deceived me with Presidency letterhead — Kalu
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Deputy Speaker Benjamin Kalu reveals how he was deceived by a ‘fake’ DG of the fictitious PFIPC, highlighting the need for stricter verification of governm
Read More: https://punchng.com/pfipc-scandal-fake-dg-deceived-me-with-presidency-letterhead-kalu/

item.title: VIDEO: Tension in Ibadan over clashes, security operatives deployed
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Tension rises in Ibadan’s Ojoo area following clashes between groups. Security operatives have been deployed to restore order. Stay updated on the Ibadan c
Read More: https://punchng.com/video-tension-in-ibadan-over-clashes-security-operatives-deployed/

item.title: Kwara urges prompt tax payment to strengthen IGR
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Kwara State Government appeals to residents for prompt tax payment to boost Internally Generated Revenue (IGR) and fund development projects.
Read More: https://punchng.com/kwara-urges-prompt-tax-payment-to-strengthen-igr/

item.title: World Cup: FBI probes Argentine FA for money laundering, fraud
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The FBI has launched a preliminary probe into the Argentine FA’s financial operations in the US, investigating potential money laundering and fraud.
Read More: https://punchng.com/world-cup-fbi-probes-argentine-fa-for-money-laundering-fraud/

item.title: Catalans' Garcia to retire at end of season and take up sporting director role
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Catalans Dragons have announced that their captain Benjamin Garcia will retire at the end of the 2026 Super League season and take up the role of sporting director at the club.

item.title: Medical experts to discuss health workers shortage, maternal deaths at colloquium
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Medical experts will discuss Nigeria’s healthcare challenges, including worker shortages, epidemics, and maternal deaths, at a colloquium in Lagos.
Read More: https://punchng.com/medical-experts-to-discuss-health-workers-shortage-maternal-deaths-at-colloquium/

item.title: Northern govs appoint ex-CDS, ex-defence minister Security Trust Fund co-chairs
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Northern governors inaugurate the Board of Trustees for the Northern Nigeria Security Trust Fund, appointing ex-CDS and ex-defence minister as co-chairs to
Read More: https://punchng.com/northern-govs-appoint-ex-cds-ex-defence-minister-security-trust-fund-co-chairs/

item.title: Senate approves Customs’ N1.3tn budget, backs N11.07tn revenue target
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Nigerian Senate has approved the Customs’ N1.3tn budget and an ambitious N11.07tn revenue target for 2026, aiming to boost non-oil revenue.
Read More: https://punchng.com/senate-approves-customs-n1-3tn-budget-backs-n11-07tn-revenue-target/

item.title: Fury vs AJ must start at 1am if fight is staged at Wembley
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: The blockbuster heavyweight clash between Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua would need to take place at 1am if it is to be held at Wembley Stadium.

item.title: YNaija Daily Recap | Top Headlines Today
item.dc:creator: YNaija
item.description: Peter Obi: “I Might Not Be Alive to Contest in 2027” Peter Obi has said he may not be alive to contest the 2027 presidential election, alleging that the Federal Government is frustrating his political activities and targeting opposition figures. Speaking during an interaction with supporters, Obi cl

item.title: Court Orders EFCC To Pay Agunloye ₦10m For Defamation
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A High Court of the Federal Capital Territory has awarded ₦10 million in damages against the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) for defaming former Minister of Power, Olu Agunloye, through publications on its website and social media platforms. Delivering judgment on Wednesday, Justice

item.title: Obi reacts to El-Rufai’s wife’s plea, calls for transparent justice
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "I woke this morning in America and was deeply moved by an emotional viral video of Hajiya Asiya El-Rufai, prompting deep reflection on our nation’s future," Obi wrote. The post Obi reacts to El-Rufai’s wife’s plea, calls for transparent justice appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Trump says ceasefire is 'over' after US and Iran trade strikes
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Speaking ahead of a Nato summit, the president says US negotiators can keep talking with the Iranians "if they want".

item.title: Liveability Index: Lagos among world’s 10 worst cities to live In – New report reveals
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The EIU Global Liveability Index is widely used by governments, businesses, and international organizations to assess living conditions in major cities. It considers factors such as safety, healthcare, education, culture, and infrastructure when evaluating the quality of life in urban areas.

item.title: Court Awards Agunloye N10m Over EFCC’s Defamatory $6bn Fraud Claim
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: A Federal Capital Territory High Court in Maitama has ordered the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) to pay N10m in damages to former Minister of Power and Steel, Dr. Olu Agunloye, after finding that the anti-graft agency defamed him by falsely linking him to a $6bn fraud. Justice Peter

item.title: Kano APC counters Kwankwasiyya over defection claims, insists members remain intact
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Kano APC dismisses Kwankwasiyya’s defection claims, insisting its members in Kumbotso LGA remain united and loyal to the party. Read more here.
Read More: https://punchng.com/kano-apc-counters-kwankwasiyya-over-defection-claims-insists-members-remain-intact/

item.title: El-Rufai: Nigeria must resist selective justice – Peter Obi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, Peter Obi, says Nigeria must must resist any hint of selective justice that erodes public trust and threatens national unity. Obi said this in a statement posted on his verified X handle on Wednesday. He was reacting to the vir

item.title: Henderson rejoins England’s World Cup squad after surgery on broken arm
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The injury has ruled the former Liverpool F.C. captain out of the remainder of the World Cup, although he will stay with Thomas Tuchel's squad in a leadership role. The post Henderson rejoins England’s World Cup squad after surgery on broken arm appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Insecurity, Poor Healthcare Push Lagos Into World’s Bottom 10 Cities
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Lagos, Nigeria’s commercial capital, has been ranked among the world’s ten least liveable cities in the Economist Intelligence Unit’s (EIU) Global Liveability Index 2026, underscoring the persistent challenges posed by insecurity, pressure on public infrastructure, healthcare defic

item.title: Henderson has surgery in USA after breaking arm during England celebration
item.dc:creator: SportingLife NG
item.description: Jordan Henderson has had surgery after breaking his arm during the celebrations of England’s win over Mexico on Sunday. The Brentford midfielder suffered the freak injury as he fell awkwardly after attempting to jump over the advertising hoardings at the side of the pitch. Henderson, who did n

item.title: Police bust eight-man robbery, cybercrime syndicate, rescue abducted boy in Gombe
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Ben Ngwakwe, Gombe The Gombe State Police Command has dismantled an eight-member criminal syndicate allegedly involved in armed robbery and cyber fraud, rescued an abducted eight-year-old boy, arrested four suspected kidnappers, and intensified its crackdown on traffic offenders and criminal hide

item.title: Middle East War Threatening Global Economic Recovery, Says IMF
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The International Monetary Fund has warned that the ongoing conflict in the Middle East poses a major threat to the global economic recovery after lowering its 2026 global growth forecast for the second time this year. In its latest outlook released on Wednesday, the IMF projected that the world eco

item.title: Nothing Ear 3a: Price, release date and full specs
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: Nothing has launched the Ear 3a, the newest earbuds in its affordable lineup. The earbuds went official on July 7, 2026, alongside the Nothing Phone 4b.

item.title: Windstorm kills 7, affects 5,403 residents in Jigawa
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: He said the disaster affected 120 communities across 13 local government areas of the state, affecting 47 public schools, three health facilities and two police stations.

item.title: Google’s Intelligent Eyewear vs Ray-Ban Meta glasses: Key differences explained
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: Meta has already put its Ray-Ban Display glasses in customers' hands. Google is now moving to challenge that dominance with Google Intelligent Eyewear, its own eyewear line, built in partnership with Samsung.

item.title: Why North Should Support Tinubu – Uba Sani
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Kaduna State Governor, Uba Sani, has reaffirmed that President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has demonstrated a strong commitment to advancing the development priorities of Northern Nigeria. He said that the Federal Government has intervened strategically across the transportation, agriculture, healthcare, educ

item.title: EU, Switzerland, AFD Reaffirm Support For Yobe’s Recovery, Resilience Programmes
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The European Union (EU), the Embassy of Switzerland, the French Development Agency (AFD) and development partners have reaffirmed their commitment to supporting durable solutions, resilience, economic recovery and water resource management in Yobe State following a joint technical mission to the Sta

item.title: Indigenous Oil Firms Say Nigeria’s Oil Industry Heavily Taxed
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Independent Petroleum Producers Group (IPPG), has called for review of taxes and levies imposed on oil industry operators in Nigeria, saying the Nigerian oil and gas industry remains the most taxed and levied in the country, and perhaps globally, with over 270 separate fees, taxes and levies. I

item.title: Plateau Assembly, PDP bicker over bill on pensions for principal officers, lawmakers
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: PDP opposes a proposed Plateau Assembly pension bill for former lawmakers, describing it as ill-timed amid security and economic challenges. The post Plateau Assembly, PDP bicker over bill on pensions for principal officers, lawmakers appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Stakeholders Seek Stronger Insurance Capacity to Deepen Oil, Gas Local Content
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Stakeholders in the Nigerian insurance industry have called for a deliberate strengthening of Nigeria’s domestic insurance capacity, saying the country can only maximise the gains of its local content policy in the oil and gas industry when more risks are underwritten by indigenous insurers. The man

item.title: Senate Advances Bill To Criminalise Drug Hawking, Others, Proposes 15-Year Jail Term
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Among its key provisions, the bill criminalises the production, importation, manufacture, transportation, distribution, sale, possession and facilitation of counterfeit medical products, fake drugs and unwholesome processed foods. The post Senate Advances Bill To Criminalise Drug Hawking, Others, Pr

item.title: ‘Shoot terrorists first; don’t wait for orders,’ defence minister tells troops
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "Once you are deployed, do not wait for any order from anybody to shoot any bandit or any terrorist," he said. The post ‘Shoot terrorists first; don’t wait for orders,’ defence minister tells troops appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Court Remands ABU Lecturer Over Alleged False Hajj Claim Against Katsina Govt
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A Chief Magistrate Court in Katsina has ordered the remand of an Ahmadu Bello University (ABU) lecturer and public affairs commentator, Dr. Bashir Kurfi, over allegations that he falsely claimed the Katsina State Government sponsored Hajj pilgrimage for some bandit leaders. Dr. Kurfi was arraigned o

item.title: Senate Approves Customs 2026 Budget, Backs N11.07trn Revenue Target
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Senate, on Wednesday, approved the 2026 budget proposal of the Nigeria Customs Service (NSC), backing a record revenue target of N11.074 trillion and total expenditure of N1.295 trillion. The approval followed consideration of the report of the Senate Committee on Customs, Excise and Tariffs, pr

item.title: SWAN Urges Fubara To Restructure State Sports Clubs, Appoint Sports Commissioner
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Sports Writers Association of Nigeria (SWAN), Rivers State chapter, has urged Governor Siminalayi Fubara to overhaul the management of government-owned sports clubs and appoint a substantive leadership for the State Ministry of Sports to revitalise sports development in the state. The associatio

item.title: Egypt FA want officials kicked out of World Cup after Argentina clash
item.dc:creator: SportingLife NG
item.description: The Egyptian Football Association says it has asked Fifa to kick the officials in charge of their World Cup last-16 defeat by Argentina out of the tournament. The EFA said it had lodged a complaint with football’s world governing body and wants an investigation into “double standards&#82

item.title: Peter Obi moved by El Rufai’s wife’s tears, demands fair treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The frontline opposition leader and the Presidential Candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Peter Obi, has called for fair treatment of the former Governor of Kaduna State, Nasir El Rufai, in the interest of justice and unity in the country. The post Peter Obi moved by El Rufai’s

item.title: Nigeria, IOM push stronger migration governance to curb irregular migration
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: …vows stronger protection for migrants The federal government and the International Organisation for Migration (IOM) have called for greater investment read more Nigeria, IOM push stronger migration governance to curb irregular migration

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …Reaffirms Commitment To Peace And Security ​The Governor of Delta State, Rt. Hon. Sheriff Oborevwori on Wednesday handed over five brand-new operational vehicles (“Green Marias”) to the Delta State Command of the Nigerian Correctional Service. The presentation, which took place at

item.title: Report raises concerns over neglect of children in budget allocations for crisis-hit communities
item.dc:creator: ICIR Nigeria
item.description: A NEW report has revealed that millions of Nigerian children affected by conflict, displacement, climate shocks and economic hardship remain largely invisible in government budgets, leaving critical early childhood services severely underfunded despite worsening humanitarian needs. The report, Finan

item.title: Benue’s Windfall: Tinubu’s reforms delivering visible gains in roads, health and agriculture – Governor
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Governor of Benue State Hyacinth Alia, on Wednesday said President Bola Tinubu’s economic reforms are beginning to deliver visible gains in the state, with improved federal allocations helping his administration fund roads, healthcare, agriculture and industrial revival on a scale he says was previo

item.title: World Cup: Referees were ‘unfair’ – Egypt’s forward speaks after Argentina loss
item.dc:creator: SportingLife NG
item.description: Egypt forward Mostafa Ziko has backed head coach Hossam Hassan’s claim that the 2026 FIFA World Cup is being “directed towards Argentina” following the Pharaohs’ dramatic 3-2 defeat to the reigning champions in the Round of 16. Egypt appeared set to pull off one of the bigges

item.title: Vessels owners to assist FG fight sea piracy, illegal oil bunkering
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Innocent Anaba In a bid to assist the Federal Government and security agencies in tackling issues of sea piracy and illegal oil bunkering along the waterways, the Tugboat, Fuel Barges, and Seagoing Vessel Association of Nigeria has called for the immediate censorship, verification, and stricter c

item.title: ADC takes control of party litigation
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has ordered all state chapters and officers nationwide to immediately forward any court documents served on the party to the National Legal Adviser in Abuja. In a public notice signed by National Publicity Secretary, Hon. Bolaji Abdullahi, the party warned that

item.title: Aviation Unions Back Commercialisation, Privatisation Of NAMA To Modernise Air Navigation
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Joint Action Committee (JAC) of unions at the Nigerian Civil Aviation Authority (NCAA) has called for the commercialisation or privatisation of the Nigerian Airspace Management Agency (NAMA), arguing that the move is necessary to modernise Nigeria’s air navigation infrastructure and improv

item.title: Man sets wheelchair user in flames with Molotov cocktail
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US police have released video showing the moment a man set a wheelchair user on fire.

item.title: Senate proposes 15-year jail term for drug hawking
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Senate has proposed a 15-year jail term for the offences of manufacturing, sale, and trafficking in fake and The post Senate proposes 15-year jail term for drug hawking appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Zlatko Dalić Steps Down As Croatia Coach After Nine Years
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Zlatko Dalić leaves Croatia national team after successful nine years Croatia have officially announced that head coach Zlatko Dalić is stepping down after almost nine years in charge. Dalić, who took over in 2017, guided the team to some of the greatest achievements in the nation’s football history

item.title: North not anyone’s political estate – Kwankwasiyya to Ali Modu Sheriff
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Kwankwasiyya Movement has criticised former Borno State Governor, Ali Modu Sheriff, over his recent comments suggesting that Peter Obi has no meaningful political future in Northern Nigeria. In a statement issued on Wednesday by its spokesperson, Habibu Sale Mohammed Mailemo, the movem

item.title: Putellas has power to change the WSL - why is she joining London City?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: London City Lionesses made a sensational bid for Alexia Putellas earlier this summer, and finally, a deal has been struck. The two-time Ballon d’Or winner is theirs. How have they managed to pull off one of the biggest shock signings of the summer - or indeed ever?

item.title: Messi headlines MLS All-Star Game roster featuring 13 FIFA World Cup stars
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Lionel Messi will headline the 2026 Major League Soccer (MLS) All-Star Game after being named among 13 FIFA World Cup players selected for the showcase event. The post Messi headlines MLS All-Star Game roster featuring 13 FIFA World Cup stars appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Northern governors, traditional rulers back state police
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The forum commended President Bola Ahmed Tinubu for prioritising security and reaffirmed support for ongoing federal efforts to restore peace nationwide.

item.title: Northern governors, traditional rulers back state police
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Northern governors and traditional rulers have reaffirmed their commitment to tackling insecurity through stronger regional collaboration and support for federal security efforts. The post Northern governors, traditional rulers back state police appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Alia backs state police, says Benue 100% compliant on LG autonomy
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Governor Hyacinth Alia says Benue is fully compliant with local government autonomy and reiterates support for state policing. The post Alia backs state police, says Benue 100% compliant on LG autonomy appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Join the military, Chief of Army Staff urges South East
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Chief of Army Staff Lt Gen Waidi Shaibu urges South East youths to join the military as the region's recruitment quota remains underutilised. The post Join the military, Chief of Army Staff urges South East appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Are Argentina being treated favourably at World Cup?
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Egypt have alleged bias in favour of Argentina and favouritism towards their star player, Lionel Messi. Is this fair?

item.title: Canadian Banks Too Rich After Searing 66% Run, Jefferies Says
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Canada’s biggest bank stocks are running out of runway as valuations have fully priced in future growth, according to analysts with Jefferies Securities Inc.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The OK Movement says political participation in Nigeria’s democracy must be protected and not hindered. The Movement made this remark in a post on its verified X handle on Wednesday. It was reacting to the statement made by the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, presidential candidate, Pet

item.title: Oil prices touch two-week highs after Trump suggests U.S.-Iran cease-fire is over
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Oil prices touched their highest levels in more than two weeks on Wednesday after President Donald Trump said he believed the cease-fire with Iran was over.

item.title: Bassey, Iwobi get new manager as Fulham appoint Arbeloa
item.dc:creator: SportingLife NG
item.description: Nigeria internationals Calvin Bassey and Alex Iwobi will work under a new manager next season after Fulham appointed Alvaro Arbeloa as their new head coach. According to Sky Sports, the former Real Madrid manager has signed a three-year contract at Craven Cottage, succeeding Marco Silva, who left th

item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has directed all its state chapters and officials nationwide to refer any court processes served on the party to the National Legal Adviser. ADC, in a statement issued by the National Publicity Secretary, Mallam Bolaji Abdullahi, also warned that no state chapte

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets

She also breaks down NATO's strategy for managing Tru

item.description: Legal practitioners have been urged to embrace collaboration over competition and focus on building enduring institutions if Africa is to read more Legal practitioners urged to prioritise collaboration as Africa’s investment landscape evolves

item.title: Belgium’s Onana Ruled Out Of World Cup With Torn ACL
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Belgium midfielder Amadou Onana has been ruled out of the remainder of the World Cup after scans confirmed a torn anterior cruciate ligament in his right knee, the Belgian Football Federation announced. The 24-year-old Aston Villa player sustained the injury early in Belgium’s 4-1 last-16 vict

Governor Otti stated this Wednesday in Aba, the state commercial hub during the Nigeria Business Summit – Regio

item.title: Regime supporters and opposition share AI-generated images of Khamenei’s funeral
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Both supporters of the Iranian regime and members of the opposition have been sharing AI-generated images of funeral services held in early July for Iran's late supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei – killed during a US-Israeli strike on February 28.

item.title: Formal Education Is Not Enough for Success, Says Jim lyke
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Nollywood actor Jim Iyke has said that true success is shaped more by life experience, mentorship and survival skills than by formal education alone. item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Benue State Governor, Hyacinth Alia, has declared that security has improved tremendously in Benue State in the last three years under the present administration. He gave the assessment on Wednesday while speaking with the State House Correspondents. He attributed the positive narratives on the stat

item.title: IMF Says AI Offsets Middle East Fallout
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The International Monetary Fund left its global growth forecast largely unchanged for this year, saying that the boom in artificial intelligence helped offset the fallout from conflict in the Middle East. Petya Koeva Brooks, Deputy Director of the IMF's Research Department, discusses the updated Wor

item.description: The Senate on Wednesday confirmed the appointment of Dr Zainab Marwa as a member of the Governing Board of the Niger Delta Development Commission, NDDC, representing North-East geo-political zone. Marwa’s confirmation followed the presentation and adoption of the report of Committee on NDD

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Edo State Command of the Nigeria Security and Civil Defence Corps (NSCDC) has deployed 1,300 personnel across the state’s three senatorial districts to provide security during the Local Government election scheduled for Saturday, July 11, 2026. The deployment was announced in a statement i

item.title: Big Tech’s $25 Billion Mega Bond Sales Are Pushing Market Limits
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Once a rare event reserved for historic mega-acquisitions, the jumbo bond sale is increasingly becoming the tech sector’s go-to financing tool. Debt offerings of $25 billion or more have quickly shifted into a baseline for tech giants funding their artificial intelligence expansion, with Amazon.com

item.title: Nigeria boosts investors’ confidence to secure its future as global energy hub
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: From Femi Folaranmi, Yenagoa   The Minister of State for Petroleum Resources (Oil), Sen Heineken Lokpobiri, has declared that Nigeria’s ongoing reforms in the petroleum sector are yielding tangible results, with increased production, rising investor confidence, and renewed global interest

item.title: NDLEA Appeals Acquittal Of Pretty Mike For Drug-Related Offences
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) has filed a notice of appeal against the ruling of Justice Abrose Lewis-Allagoa of a Federal High Court in Lagos, which cleared a socialite and nightclub owner, Mike Nwalie, popularly known as Pretty Mike, of drug-related offences. The judge had, on t

item.title: Onitsha traders hand over N435m worth of substandard drugs to NAFDAC
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Vincent Ujumadu Traders at the Bridge Head Drug Market in Onitsha have handed over substandard, falsified and expired drugs valued at N435 million to the National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC) for destruction. The Chairman of the market’s Caretaker Committee,

item.title: Wizkid associate, Godson denies fraud allegation, gives own account of business with ISB
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Popular businessman and social media influencer, Uyi Godson Agho, also known as Godson, with the Instagram handle @godson45, has denied allegations that he defrauded social media influencer Clement Akpobome Ogudu, also known as IsokoBoy (ISB), of N100 million. The controversy erupted after a video c

item.title: $14,000 dog grooming bills and 25% pricier flights: How the K-shaped economy is punishing the middle class
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: UPDATED: Two feared dead, others injured as scavengers, traders clash in Ibadan
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: According to him, the rider had collected the cash, but the PoS operator could not confirm receipt of payment because of... The post UPDATED: Two feared dead, others injured as scavengers, traders clash in Ibadan appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Senate approves Customs’ 2026 budget, backs N11.074trn revenue target
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday approved the 2026 budget proposal of the Nigeria Customs Service, NCS, endorsing a revenue target of N11.074 trillion and a total expenditure of N1.295 trillion for the 2026 fiscal year. The approval followed the consideration of the report of the Senate Committee o

item.title: World Cup 2026: Nine coaches lost jobs after elimination [FULL LIST]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: At least nine coaches have lost their jobs after their teams’ elimination at the ongoing FIFA World Cup. DAILY POST reports that Croatia head coach, Zlatko Dalic, is the latest manager to step down from the role. Dalic’s departure is coming after Croatia’s recent 2-1 defeat to Port

item.title: Ex-Arsenal Defender: Alonso Needs Time To Turn Chelsea Around
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Former Arsenal defender Nacho Monreal has disclosed that Chelsea manager Xabi Alonso must be given more time to implement his tactics on the team. After a turbulent 2025/26 campaign that saw the Blues slip to ninth and miss out on European football, the hierarchy views Alonso as the ideal figure to

item.title: 2027: Peter Obi says Tinubu is tired, rules out desperation for Presidency
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Peter Obi, has said President Bola Tinubu is "tired" and should leave office after the 2027 general election. The post 2027: Peter Obi says Tinubu is tired, rules out desperation for Presidency appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Airstrikes, troops repel coordinated terrorist attacks in North-West
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Coordinated air and ground operations by troops of Operation FANSAN YAMMA have disrupted what security authorities described as a major read more Airstrikes, troops repel coordinated terrorist attacks in North-West
